template_name: Standard Prescription Format - Newborn Hearing Screening & Follow-up

condition.name: Newborn Hearing Screening (UNHS)
condition.definition: Universal screening program to detect congenital hearing loss early in neonates.
purpose: Prevent speech, language, and cognitive delay

timing.initial_screen: Before hospital discharge or by 1 month of age

risk_factors: Family history of childhood hearing loss

rescreening_guidelines.action: Repeat OAE or proceed to AABR
Scheduled diagnostic audiology even if initial screen normal

red_flags: No response to name by 6–9 months
Parental concern regarding hearing or speech

diagnosis_and_followup.action: Refer for diagnostic audiology by 2 months

parent_counseling: Screening is safe, painless, and quick — जांच सुरक्षित, दर्दरहित और जल्दी होती है।
Failure does not always mean permanent hearing loss — फेल होने का मतलब हमेशा बहरापन नहीं है।
Follow-up is essential — फॉलो-अप ज़रूरी है।
Early detection improves speech and language development — जल्दी पहचान से बोलने और भाषा के विकास में सुधार होता है।
The 1-2-3 rule: Screen by 1 month, diagnose by 2 months, intervene by 3 months — 1-2-3 नियम: 1 महीने तक जांच, 2 महीने तक पहचान, 3 महीने तक इलाज शुरू।

standard_prescription_format.title: Newborn Hearing Screening Prescription & Follow-up Sheet

warning_signs: No startle to loud sound by 1 month — 1 महीने तक तेज़ आवाज़ पर न चौंकना
No babbling by 6 months — 6 महीने तक बोलने की कोशिश न करना
No response to name by 9 months — 9 महीने तक नाम पर प्रतिक्रिया न देना
No words by 12 months — 12 महीने तक कोई शब्द न बोलना
Parent concern about hearing — माता-पिता को सुनने में संदेह
expected_course: Most newborns pass the initial hearing screening. Those who fail require rescreening within 2-4 weeks. Persistent failure leads to diagnostic audiology by 2 months of age. With confirmed hearing loss, intervention (hearing aids, cochlear implant evaluation) should begin by 3 months to optimize speech and language outcomes.
key_clinical_points: Universal newborn hearing screening (UNHS) is recommended for all newborns.
OAE screens cochlear function; AABR assesses the auditory nerve pathway.
AABR is preferred for NICU babies (risk of auditory neuropathy).
High-risk babies need periodic monitoring even if initial screen is normal.
Follow the 1-2-3 rule: Screen by 1 month, Diagnose by 2 months, Intervene by 3 months.
